classes.label: Session
classes.evidence: Each Timepoint is associated with a set of Readings which refer to the Parameter being observed and its value.

classes.label: Core Monitoring Parameter
classes.evidence: the minimum minute by minute monitoring data required for inclusion in the BrainIT Database include: Heart Rate, Respiration Rate, Mean Arterial Pressure, mean ICP, Pulse Oximetry (SaO2) and Temperature.

classes.label: Optional Monitoring Parameter
classes.evidence: EtCO2, SjO2, TCD, PbrO2, Brain Temperature, Microdialysis.

classes.label: Derived Parameter
classes.evidence: Cerebral perfusion pressure (CPP 1/4 BP-ICP) monitoring is a good example.

classes.label: Baseline Therapy
classes.evidence: baseline medical management and monitoring of clinical status that occurs to patients.

classes.label: Secondary Insult Therapy
classes.evidence: medical management=therapy given to patients specifically to treat secondary insults.

classes.label: Antibiotics
classes.evidence: antibiotics.

classes.label: Jugular Venous Desaturation
classes.evidence: jugular venous desaturations < 50%.

classes.evidence: Clinical Assessment.

classes.evidence: Routine laboratory tests performed during ICU management.

classes.evidence: Routine blood gases.

classes.evidence: White Cell Count.

classes.evidence: Haematocrit.

classes.label: Nursing Intervention
classes.evidence: Routine nursing care.

classes.label: Routine Nursing Care
classes.evidence: Routine nursing (eye=mouth care, ET tube suction, bed care).

classes.label: Physiotherapy
classes.evidence: physiotherapy.

classes.label: Bedside Intervention
classes.evidence: bedside interventions= investigations.

classes.label: Patient Transport
classes.evidence: patient transport (to and from Theatre=CT-Scan).

hierarchy.evidence: As a result, secondary insults such as intracranial hypertension, and arterial hypotension have been found to occur, in head injured patients, more frequently than previously realised with more than 90 percent of patients exhibiting one or more insults during their management in intensive care [12].
provenance: The BrainIT group concept and core dataset definition::chunk-11

hierarchy.evidence: physiotherapy.
antibiotics.
intracranial pressure monitoring.
Cerebral perfusion pressure (CPP 1/4 BP-ICP) monitoring.
Routine blood gases.
Haemaglobin.
White Cell Count.
Haematocrit.
Schema-inferred: Systemic Hypotension is a subclass of Secondary Insult.
hierarchy.justification: Systemic Hypotension is a type of Secondary Insult.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome is a subclass of Secondary Insult.
hierarchy.justification: Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome can be a Secondary Insult.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Jugular Venous Desaturation is a subclass of Secondary Insult.
hierarchy.justification: Jugular Venous Desaturation is a type of Secondary Insult.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Arterial Hypotension is a subclass of Systemic Hypotension.
hierarchy.justification: Arterial Hypotension is a specific case of Systemic Hypotension.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Ventilation is a subclass of Baseline Therapy.
hierarchy.justification: Ventilation is a type of Baseline Therapy.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Sedation is a subclass of Baseline Therapy.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Fluids is a subclass of Baseline Therapy.

hierarchy.evidence: Schema-inferred: Vasopressors is a subclass of Baseline Therapy.

hierarchy.justification: Microdialysis is an Optional Monitoring Parameter.

hierarchy.justification: Brain Temperature is an Optional Monitoring Parameter.
